title: Medellín 10-Day City Break
metaTitle: Medellín 10-Day City Break | Flights, Hotels & Tours Included
metaDescription: Book a 10-day trip to Medellín, Colombia: round-trip flights, hotel, breakfast, transfers, and guided tours including Comuna 13 graffiti tour and Metrocable to Parque Arví. Prices from $1020 per person (estimate).
overview: Spend 10 days discovering Medellín, Colombia — Known as the "City of Eternal Spring" — mild temperatures year-round (highs around 24-27°C) thanks to its valley altitude, with two rainier periods (April-May and October-November). December to March — driest months, though Medellín's "eternal spring" climate is mild and pleasant nearly year-round. This itinerary blends Comuna 13 graffiti tour, Metrocable to Parque Arví, Plaza Botero with free time to explore at your own pace, backed by round-trip flights, handpicked accommodation, and a dedicated travel consultant from enquiry through departure.
included: Round-trip economy-class airfare from your selected origin city (upgrade to premium economy or business available)
9 nights accommodation in your selected category (Budget, Mid-range, Premium, or Luxury)
Daily local transport as per the itinerary
Guided tours including Comuna 13 graffiti tour, Metrocable to Parque Arví, Plaza Botero
All entrance fees for attractions and activities listed in the itinerary
Applicable taxes and OTA service fee
Dedicated travel consultant support from booking through departure
notIncluded: Visa fees and travel document costs (see Visa Information)
Travel insurance (strongly recommended, available as an add-on)
Lunches and dinners except where noted in the itinerary
Optional activities not listed in the day-by-day itinerary
Airline seat selection and baggage upgrades
Any fare difference for premium economy or business class upgrades

itinerary.title: Arrive in Medellín
itinerary.description: Land at José María Córdova International Airport (MDE) and take your included private transfer to your hotel. After settling in, ease into the trip with a relaxed visit to Comuna 13 graffiti tour — Guided walking tour and outdoor escalators through a transformed hillside neighborhood. Round off the evening with a welcome dinner introducing you to local flavors.

itinerary.title: Medellín: Metrocable to Parque Arví & Museo de Antioquia
itinerary.description: Morning visit to Metrocable to Parque Arví — Cable-car ride over the city into a mountaintop nature reserve. In the afternoon, continue to Museo de Antioquia — Art museum with an extensive Fernando Botero collection. Evening free to explore local restaurants and nightlife.

itinerary.title: Medellín: Guatapé and El Peñol rock day trip & Pablo Escobar history tour
itinerary.description: Morning visit to Guatapé and El Peñol rock day trip — Climb the 740-step rock and visit the colorful lakeside town. In the afternoon, continue to Pablo Escobar history tour — Guided tour covering the Medellín cartel era and its aftermath. Evening free to explore local restaurants and nightlife.
activities: Guatapé and El Peñol rock day trip

itinerary.title: Day Trip to Guatapé and El Peñol
itinerary.description: Full-day excursion to Guatapé and El Peñol, a popular side trip from Medellín. Return to Medellín in the evening with time free to explore on your own.
activities: Guided day trip: Guatapé and El Peñol

itinerary.title: Leisure Day & Optional Activities
itinerary.description: A lighter day to recharge, shop, or add an optional experience. Popular choices include Cerro Nutibara and Pueblito Paisa replica village — your consultant can book add-ons like this on request.
activities: Cerro Nutibara and Pueblito Paisa replica village

itinerary.title: Medellín: Plaza Botero & El Poblado
itinerary.description: Morning visit to Plaza Botero — Public square with 23 sculptures donated by Fernando Botero. In the afternoon, continue to El Poblado — Leafy, upscale neighborhood of cafés, parks, and nightlife. Evening free to explore local restaurants and nightlife.

itinerary.title: Day Trip to Santa Fe de Antioquia colonial town
itinerary.description: Full-day excursion to Santa Fe de Antioquia colonial town, a popular side trip from Medellín. Return to Medellín in the evening with time free to explore on your own.
activities: Guided day trip: Santa Fe de Antioquia colonial town

itinerary.title: Medellín: Jardín Botánico de Medellín & Parque Lleras
itinerary.description: Morning visit to Jardín Botánico de Medellín — Free botanical garden with a butterfly house. In the afternoon, continue to Parque Lleras — Central plaza in El Poblado surrounded by bars and restaurants. Evening free to explore local restaurants and nightlife.

itinerary.title: Leisure Day & Optional Activities
itinerary.description: A lighter day to recharge, shop, or add an optional experience. Popular choices include Barrio Colombia design district — your consultant can book add-ons like this on request.

itinerary.title: Departure Day
itinerary.description: Enjoy a final morning at leisure — perhaps one last visit to Comuna 13 graffiti tour or some souvenir shopping — before your private transfer to MDE for your departure flight.

travelTips: Take the Metrocable gondola just for the ride — it doubles as a scenic city viewpoint on the way to Parque Arví.
Book a Comuna 13 tour with a local guide for context on the neighborhood's transformation, not just photos.
Guatapé is an easy full-day trip from Medellín — go early to beat both crowds and afternoon rain.
The Metro is safe, cheap, and the fastest way to get around during the day.
Pace yourself — this itinerary includes free time so you are not rushing between every attraction.
packingList: Copies of booking confirmations and travel insurance
Light layers — mornings can be cool, afternoons mild and warm
A light rain jacket for the two rainier seasons
Comfortable shoes for hilly neighborhoods like Comuna 13
thingsToDo: Guatapé and El Peñol rock day trip
Climbing the 740 steps of El Peñol rock
Parque Arví nature reserve via cable car
thingsToAvoid: Framing conversations or tours purely around cartel history — locals prefer the city's transformation to take center stage.
Walking alone in unfamiliar comunas after dark.
Skipping sunscreen — the valley altitude means stronger UV than the mild temperatures suggest.
topRestaurants: El Cielo — Avant-garde Colombian tasting menu ($$$$)
hiddenGems: Cerro Nutibara and Pueblito Paisa replica village
Jardín (nearby colorful coffee town day trip)
familyActivities: Parque Arví nature reserve via cable car
adventureActivities: Climbing the 740 steps of El Peñol rock
luxuryExperiences: Private coffee-farm tour with tasting in the nearby countryside
Suite stay at a boutique El Poblado hotel
Private Guatapé and El Peñol day tour with driver
honeymoonExperiences: Private day trip to Guatapé's lake
Cable car ride over the city at dusk
localEtiquette: Paisas (Medellín locals) are known for warmth — a handshake or light cheek kiss on greeting is common.
Dress reasonably smart-casual; Medellín is more fashion-conscious than many South American cities.
Referencing the city's history sensitively — locals appreciate its transformation being recognized, not just its past.
localTransportGuide: Medellín's Metro (with connecting Metrocable gondola lines up the hillside comunas) is clean, efficient, and widely considered the best public transit in Colombia — a genuine point of local pride. Rideshare apps are also easy and inexpensive for door-to-door trips.
bestTimeToVisit: December to March — driest months, though Medellín's "eternal spring" climate is mild and pleasant nearly year-round.
weather: Known as the "City of Eternal Spring" — mild temperatures year-round (highs around 24-27°C) thanks to its valley altitude, with two rainier periods (April-May and October-November).
visaInformation: Visa-free entry for stays up to 90 days for US, UK, EU, Canadian, and Australian passport holders. Indian nationals need a Colombian e-visa arranged in advance.
averageFlightTime: ~16.9h from major hubs (estimate; varies by origin — see Suggested Flights)
cancellationPolicy: Cancellations made 30+ days before departure: full refund minus a $99 processing fee. 15-29 days before departure: 50% refund. 0-14 days before departure or no-show: non-refundable. Airline and hotel supplier cancellation terms may be stricter than this policy and apply in addition — your travel consultant confirms exact terms before payment is taken. No online payment is collected on this site; a consultant confirms final pricing and availability with you directly before any booking is confirmed.
termsAndConditions: Pricing shown is per person based on the traveler type and tier selected and is an estimate pending live fare and rate confirmation.
Passports must be valid for at least 6 months beyond the return date for most destinations — confirm the exact requirement for your nationality.
Itinerary order may change due to weather, local conditions, or attraction closures; inclusions remain equivalent.
Solo travelers incur a single-room supplement, reflected in the Solo pricing tier.
This package does not include international travel insurance unless explicitly listed under Included.
Prices shown in a currency other than USD are approximate conversions at the rate noted and are not a payment quote.

faqs.question: Do I need a visa to visit Medellín?
faqs.answer: Visa-free entry for stays up to 90 days for US, UK, EU, Canadian, and Australian passport holders. Indian nationals need a Colombian e-visa arranged in advance.

faqs.question: What is the best time to visit Medellín?
faqs.answer: December to March — driest months, though Medellín's "eternal spring" climate is mild and pleasant nearly year-round. Known as the "City of Eternal Spring" — mild temperatures year-round (highs around 24-27°C) thanks to its valley altitude, with two rainier periods (April-May and October-November).

faqs.question: What's included in this package?
faqs.answer: Round-trip airfare, accommodation, daily breakfast, airport transfers, daily local transport, and guided tours to the attractions listed in the itinerary. See the Included / Not Included sections for full detail.

faqs.question: Can the itinerary be customized?
faqs.answer: Yes — this itinerary is a starting point. Your travel consultant can add, remove, or reorder activities, extend your stay, or upgrade your hotel category before you book.

faqs.question: What is the cancellation policy?
faqs.answer: Cancellations made 30+ days before departure: full refund minus a $99 processing fee. 15-29 days before departure: 50% refund. 0-14 days before departure or no-show: non-refundable. Airline and hotel supplier cancellation terms may be stricter than this policy and apply in addition — your travel consultant confirms exact terms before payment is taken. No online payment is collected on this site; a consultant confirms final pricing and availability with you directly before any booking is confirmed.

faqs.question: Is Medellín safe for tourists?
faqs.answer: Medellín is generally safe for tourists who take standard precautions — avoid poorly lit areas at night, keep valuables secure, and use licensed transport.

faqs.question: What currency should I bring to Medellín?
faqs.answer: The local currency is COP. Cards and mobile payments are widely accepted, and ATMs are easy to find.

faqs.question: Is this package good for a honeymoon or family trip?
faqs.answer: This itinerary suits couples, solo travelers, and small groups equally well — talk to your consultant about tailoring it further.

reviews.author: Noah Larsen
reviews.title: Five stars, will return
reviews.body: Our trip to Medellín was fantastic from start to finish. The hotel was clean and well located, and the guided visit to El Poblado was a real highlight. Airport transfers were on time both ways.

reviews.author: Ava Novak
reviews.title: Perfect for our honeymoon
reviews.body: Jardín Botánico de Medellín alone was worth the trip, but combined with Plaza Botero this itinerary really delivered. Breakfast at the hotel was good every day and the local guide was knowledgeable.

reviews.author: Lukas Khan
reviews.title: Would book again in a heartbeat
reviews.body: Medellín is stunning and this package covered the essentials well, especially Pablo Escobar history tour. The hotel could have been a little closer to the center but transport was easy.
